name: snowflake
description: Query and manage your Snowflake data warehouse with SQL operations across databases, schemas, and tables

envVars.name: SNOWFLAKE_WAREHOUSE
envVars.description: Default warehouse to use for queries (e.g., COMPUTE_WH)

envVars.name: SNOWFLAKE_DATABASE
envVars.description: Default database to use for queries

envVars.name: SNOWFLAKE_SCHEMA
envVars.description: Default schema to use for queries (defaults to PUBLIC)

tools.name: Run Query
tools.description: Execute a SQL query against your Snowflake data warehouse

tools.name: List Databases
tools.description: List all databases in your Snowflake account

tools.name: List Schemas
tools.description: List all schemas in a Snowflake database

tools.name: List Tables
tools.description: List all tables in a Snowflake database schema

tools.name: Describe Table
tools.description: Get detailed column information for a specific table

prompts.title: Query my data warehouse
prompts.prompt: Help me query data from my Snowflake data warehouse. Show me specific records or analyze patterns.

prompts.title: Analyze table structure
prompts.prompt: Show me the structure of tables in my Snowflake database and help me understand the schema.

prompts.title: Generate insights
prompts.prompt: Analyze my Snowflake data and generate insights about trends, patterns, and anomalies.

prompts.title: Optimize queries
prompts.prompt: Help me optimize my SQL queries for better performance in Snowflake.

steps.title: Locate Your Account Identifier
steps.description: Find your Snowflake account identifier in the Snowflake web interface URL (e.g., xy12345.us-east-1 from https://xy12345.us-east-1.snowflakecomputing.com)

steps.title: Create or Use Existing User
steps.description: Use an existing Snowflake user or create a new one with appropriate permissions. The user needs USAGE privileges on the warehouse and database, and SELECT privileges on tables.

steps.title: Configure Warehouse
steps.description: Ensure you have a warehouse created and running. The default warehouse is typically named COMPUTE_WH.

steps.title: Set Environment Variables
steps.description: Add the following environment variables to your .env file with your Snowflake credentials
steps.code:
SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT=xy12345.us-east-1
SNOWFLAKE_USERNAME=your_username
SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD=your_password
SNOWFLAKE_WAREHOUSE=COMPUTE_WH
SNOWFLAKE_DATABASE=your_database
SNOWFLAKE_SCHEMA=PUBLIC

steps.title: Test Connection
steps.description: Test your connection by listing databases or running a simple query
